exact_pattern: 15-20 min warmup; 5 x 1500m controlled-fast; 500m easy jog after each; 10-15 min cooldown
watch_executable_shape: distance repeats with explicit recovery distance
primary_stimulus: 10K-specific speed endurance; high aerobic power; repeatable race-like rhythm
use_when: runner already handles one hard session well and can recover after long runs; coach wants strong 10K or sharp half-marathon stimulus
avoid_when: beginner_new_runner; sometimes_runs; conservative Marathon Base; low-support runners
why_it_matters: This is the closest Hito analogue to the uploaded Suunto session and is a real performance workout, not a generic interval day
why_missing_or_underused_now: Current Hito intervals are broader and safer: roughly 400m-1000m repeats, with no dedicated long-interval identity for 1500m repeats

exact_pattern: 15 min warmup; 4 x 1600m controlled-fast; 2 min easy jog; 10 min cooldown
watch_executable_shape: distance repeats with explicit recovery time
use_when: runner needs durable race-rhythm support without going full VO2 style
avoid_when: beginner_new_runner; sometimes_runs weak-support; early-base Marathon Base
why_it_matters: This is a strong bridge between threshold and classic 10K intervals
why_missing_or_underused_now: Hito has a generic long-interval concept but no dedicated mile-repeat or near-mile specific identity

exact_pattern: 15-20 min warmup; 4 x 2km controlled threshold-like; 2 min easy jog; 10-15 min cooldown
watch_executable_shape: distance repeats with explicit recovery time
primary_stimulus: Threshold durability; half-marathon specific aerobic strength
use_when: runner is preparing for Half and needs longer threshold durability than short tempo blocks
avoid_when: 10K beginner paths; low-support Marathon Base; any runner without stable volume
why_it_matters: This is much more half-specific than generic tempo repeats
why_missing_or_underused_now: Current Hito threshold work is time-based and compact; it does not yet express long cruise-interval variants

exact_pattern: 20 min warmup; 3 x 3km threshold-like; 1km controlled float between reps; 10-15 min cooldown
primary_stimulus: High-end threshold durability and controlled clearance under fatigue
use_when: experienced runner can handle floats and needs long sustained quality without pure race pace
avoid_when: all beginner and mid-support plans; conservative load contexts
why_it_matters: This is a hallmark advanced endurance workout and a much richer identity than generic threshold blocks
why_missing_or_underused_now: Hito currently avoids float recoveries and keeps recovery explicit/easy for safety and simplicity

display_name: Broken tempo 3 x 12 min / 2 min jog
exact_pattern: 15 min warmup; 3 x 12 min controlled tempo; 2 min easy jog; 10 min cooldown
watch_executable_shape: time repeats with explicit recovery time
primary_stimulus: Tempo durability; safe aerobic strength progression
use_when: runner is beyond beginner adaptation and needs more durable tempo than 2 x 8-10 min
avoid_when: true beginner plans; heavy conservative contexts if recovery is weak
why_it_matters: This is a practical progression from the current Hito tempo template and stays safer than sharper interval work
why_missing_or_underused_now: Hito tempo exists, but it is still generic and not expressed as a richer progression ladder with named variants

exact_pattern: 15 min warmup; 6 x 1000m controlled-fast; 200m easy jog; 10 min cooldown
watch_executable_shape: distance repeats with explicit recovery distance
use_when: runner is on a real 10K path and needs more specific rhythm than short 400m reps
avoid_when: Half beginner scenarios; Marathon Base; low-support runners
why_it_matters: This is one of the most recognizable and executable 10K workouts
why_missing_or_underused_now: Hito can approximate it through generic intervals, but does not own it as a dedicated 10K-specific identity

exact_pattern: 15 min warmup; 5 x 1200m controlled-fast; 200m easy jog; 10 min cooldown
watch_executable_shape: distance repeats with explicit recovery distance
primary_stimulus: 10K-specific strength with slightly longer reps than 1km
use_when: runner needs a step up from 1km reps without jumping to 1500m repeats
why_it_matters: This is a useful middle rung between generic intervals and very demanding long intervals
why_missing_or_underused_now: Current library jumps from broad intervals to general long intervals without enough named middle-distance repeat variants

exact_pattern: 15-20 min warmup; 5 x 1000m hard-controlled; 2 min easy jog; 10-15 min cooldown
watch_executable_shape: distance repeats with explicit recovery time
use_when: runner is already robust and the product intentionally supports a sharper performance track
avoid_when: current deterministic normal path; beginner/recreational runners; Marathon Base; normal Half completion flows
why_it_matters: This is a real quality session but should remain out of broad v1 distribution
why_missing_or_underused_now: Hito correctly avoids this today because the normal engine is intentionally conservative and not an advanced two-quality system

display_name: Hill strength 8 x 90 sec uphill / jog down
exact_pattern: 15 min warmup; 8 x 90 sec uphill; easy jog or walk/jog down; 10 min cooldown
watch_executable_shape: time repeats with explicit downhill or jog recoveries
primary_stimulus: Strength endurance; climbing economy; mechanics under force
use_when: runner needs a richer hill session than short 45 sec repeat work
avoid_when: flat-only plans with no hill access; beginner runners; recovery-compromised weeks
why_it_matters: This is a more serious hill workout than the current short-repeat template and is common in strong plans
why_missing_or_underused_now: Hito hills are currently a single short-repeat pattern and do not express longer hill-strength variants

exact_pattern: 15-20 min warmup; 3 x 3km controlled threshold-half specific; 90 sec easy jog; 10-15 min cooldown
watch_executable_shape: distance repeats with short explicit recovery
primary_stimulus: Half-marathon durability and pace resistance without fake pace targets
use_when: runner needs clear half-specific work beyond generic tempo and short intervals
avoid_when: sometimes_runs half with weaker support; beginner half contexts
why_it_matters: This would solve the current Hito gap where supported Half can still feel too generic
why_missing_or_underused_now: Current Hito Half library is improving but still leans too heavily on generic tempo or compact threshold blocks

exact_pattern: 20 min warmup; 3 x 5km steady-durable; 1km easy between blocks; 10-15 min cooldown
watch_executable_shape: distance blocks with explicit easy resets
primary_stimulus: Marathon-specific steady durability without pretending race-pace truth
typical_phase: Late Build; Specific in future Marathon family, not current Marathon Base v1
use_when: experienced runner has strong support and the product later opens a true marathon-specific family
avoid_when: current Marathon Base v1; beginners; low-support target-time requests
why_it_matters: This is a real marathon-specific workout identity, but not appropriate for current base-only Hito promise
why_missing_or_underused_now: Hito Marathon Base is intentionally not a full Marathon training family yet, so this should remain reference-only for now

display_name: Progression long run with last 30 min steady
exact_pattern: 10 min opener; long easy main block; final 30 min steady-durable; 5-10 min easy cooldown
watch_executable_shape: time-based long run with explicit finish block
primary_stimulus: Long-run specificity; fatigue resistance; steady finish control
best_goal_families: Half Marathon; Marathon future full family
use_when: runner already tolerates long easy volume and needs a more expressive long-run finish
avoid_when: beginner_new_runner; conservative heavy contexts; early-base weeks
why_it_matters: This is a much richer long-run identity than anonymous long aerobic time
why_missing_or_underused_now: Current Hito long runs are better than before, but finish-specific long-run variants are still underrepresented

exact_pattern: 20 min warmup; 4 rounds of 2km threshold-like plus 1km steady float; 10-15 min cooldown
watch_executable_shape: alternating distance blocks with float structure
primary_stimulus: Advanced aerobic durability and pace-change control
best_goal_families: Half Marathon; Marathon future advanced-safe family
use_when: runner is advanced enough to use alternating blocks without breaking form or overreaching
avoid_when: normal deterministic v1 path; beginners; low-support runners
why_it_matters: This is a high-quality endurance workout and a clear example of the kind of rich session Hito does not yet express
why_missing_or_underused_now: It is too advanced and too nuanced for the current one-touch conservative engine, but useful as future source truth
